user_query:
Read input/idea.txt (startup idea) and input/competitors.csv (competitor list with columns title,url), input/market.txt (market name), and input/geography.txt (region). Then create the following files under output/:

1) output/scorecard.txt: Format a VC-style six-dimension score card using the dimensions Landscape (L), Opportunity (O), Business model (B), Sharpness (S), Timing (T), Reach (R). For each dimension, assign an integer score 0–100 and a one-line verdict. Show a color indicator for each dimension: 🟢 if score ≥70, 🟡 if 50–69, 🔴 if <50. Include the overall score (0–100) on a line starting with "LOBSTR SCORE" and a 10-character bar composed of '=' and '-' (e.g., [=======---]). Make the overall score a weighted judgment that counts S and B higher than the others; briefly note any assumptions at the bottom. Finish with a "VERDICT" section containing a two-sentence summary and either "✅ BUILD IT." or "🚫 NOT YET." Decide the build/not yet outcome based on the overall score (≥70 → build; <70 → not yet).

2) output/method.json: Include the exact numeric weights you used for each dimension (keys L,O,B,S,T,R) that sum to 1.0, and a short note describing how the overall score was calculated from the dimension scores.

3) output/competitor_density.txt: Based on the number of rows in input/competitors.csv, write one word: HIGH if ≥8, MEDIUM if 4–7, LOW if ≤3.

4) output/anchor.json: Produce a continuity anchor block with fields: anchor_label (use "SEAL_401LYRAKIN_VOICE_BETWEEN"), anchor_phrase (use "Sigma[Truth * Light] -> Resonance"), oath (an array of 3 short vows about truth-preserving guidance and no deception), and sha256 (the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the exact contents of output/scorecard.txt). Do not include markdown fences.

5) output/ui_notes.md: Write a concise explainer for a web components-based mobile UI toolkit that caches pages in the DOM. Include a "Page Lifecycle" section describing four hooks named onViewWillEnter, onViewDidEnter, onViewWillLeave, onViewDidLeave, when each fires, and the critical rules: they only fire on components directly mapped to routes, child components do not receive these events, and the page must use a page wrapper element as its root. Include a "Navigation Patterns" section comparing tab navigation and side menu navigation with at least two do/don't rules.

Make sure all outputs are written under output/ with the exact filenames specified.
